scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Fits-but-mis-framed: the narrated create-form/fill moment is gone — the settled still jumped past it to the post-create 'Manage Solicitations' list. The video clip shows scroll+fill+click correctly, but the deck/screenshot still-frame lands on the result, not the act.
fix_recommendation: Settle the scene-2 capture ON the create form (Coverage-areas panel + filled Description visible). Keep the create click (must_succeed), but capture the form as the settled frame rather than letting the post-create redirect carry the still to the manage list. Walkthrough-scripting fix.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The snapshotted Coverage-areas concept (S4) is invisible in the settled frame because it shows the manage list, not the create form panel.
fix_recommendation: Same as the motion_friction fix — settle on the create form so the Coverage-areas panel (the concept's load-bearing artifact) is in frame.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: The manage-list frame shows no coverage artifact, so the 'exact coverage the firm will bid on' claim is ungrounded in that frame.
fix_recommendation: Settle on the create form Coverage-areas panel so the coverage is visibly grounded.

scene: 2 — Maya finds the plan already waiting as the coverage area
detail: Two near-duplicate 'R6 — Attakar × Gura' rows (new Draft + canonical Active) appear in the manage list with no disambiguator.
fix_recommendation: Disambiguate the freshly-created Draft row from the canonical Active call (distinct title, 'new' indicator) — OR avoid showing the list at all by settling on the create form (preferred; subsumed by the framing fix).

scene: 5 — Maya awards the firm and sees it was notified
detail: The contact email / notification timestamp secondary text sits in a low-contrast grey-on-white band; may fail WCAG AA.
fix_recommendation: Darken the muted secondary text (contact email, timestamp) on the award confirmation to meet WCAG AA (>=4.5:1).
